texto: A cultured society that has fallen away from its religious traditions expects more from art than the aesthetic consciousness and the 'standpoint of art' can deliver. The Romantic desire for a new mythology... gives the artist and his task in the world the consciousness of a new consecration. He is something like a 'secular saviour' for his creations are expected to achieve on a small scale the propitiation of disaster for which an unsaved world hopes.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hans-Georg Gadamer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “A cultured society that has fallen away from its religious traditions expects more from art than …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, art, consciousness pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de consciousness e religion.

texto: Long before we understand ourselves through the process of self-examination, we understand ourselves in a self-evident way in the family, society and state in which we live.
explicacao: Citação de Hans-Georg Gadamer sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Long before we understand ourselves through the process of self-examination, we understand oursel…” articula familia, society, live em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como estudos e live.

texto: What man needs is not just the persistent posing of ultimate questions, but the sense of what is feasible, what is possible, what is correct, here and now. The philosopher, of all people, must, I think, be aware of the tension between what he claims to achieve and the reality in which he finds himself.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Hans-Georg Gadamer sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “What man needs is not just the persistent posing of ultimate questions, but the sense of what is …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, philosophy, needs, útil em momentos de reflexao.
